findingDetails.title: The new usage wrapper presents an unconstrained dynamic script loader as an implementation pattern
findingDetails.evidence: The packet-added OVERVIEW recommends applying the documented loading patterns while loadScript(src) assigns an unconstrained caller value to script.src without a trusted-static-URL contract, HTTPS origin allowlist, or integrity metadata; the companion third-party reference demonstrates those controls.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact scoped searches found this generic unconstrained loader and a separate allowlisted implementation; inspected shell, DOM-sink, cookie, and secret examples were explicitly marked dangerous or bounded to non-sensitive data.
findingDetails.recommendation: State that src must be a trusted static URL, or validate HTTPS against an origin allowlist and add integrity metadata where available; link the safe third-party loader pattern.

traceabilityChecks.result: The scope is documentation-only with no executable capability, but one packet-added usage wrapper creates a P2 recommendation-framing advisory.
Packet evidence says body content was preserved; no pre-change baseline was replayed, so the active issue is limited to current wrapper framing.

noveltyJustification: One new P2 isolates an in-scope security-semantic effect of the added usage wrapper around a pre-existing generic script-loader body; known unrelated cookie and third-party concerns were directly ruled out as current defects rather than re-reported.
edgeCases: The implementation summary's third-party arbitrary-input concern is stale against the current allowlisted implementation and is deferred to traceability.
The disclosed cookie contradiction is no longer active because client-set non-sensitive cookies and server-set HttpOnly session cookies are now distinguished.
Historical body provenance was not replayed; severity remains P2 because no executable path or untrusted caller is evidenced.

ruledOut.candidate: P0/P1 escalation for the resource loader
ruledOut.reason: Only a static example caller is shown, no untrusted caller is evidenced, and the target is documentation-only.
Current guidance distinguishes non-sensitive client cookies from HttpOnly server-set session cookies.

ruledOut.candidate: Active arbitrary-input defect in the third-party best-practices loader
ruledOut.reason: Current code parses HTTPS URLs, allowlists hosts, and notes SRI.

ruledOut.candidate: Unsafe eval, shell, DOM-sink, and secret examples presented as recommendations
ruledOut.reason: Focused reads show explicit dangerous/bad labels and safer alternatives.
Not applicable to a Markdown corpus/current-tree review; graphless exact-search and direct-read evidence covered selected security classes.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: The non-trivial documentation corpus contains implementation examples that cross script, DOM, cookie, shell, and secret trust boundaries.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Packet claims establish the changed wrapper boundary; exact sink searches selected the highest-risk implementation examples for direct framing and trust-boundary adjudication.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A packet-added wrapper can present a pre-existing unsafe script-loader body as recommended implementation guidance.

searchActions.queryOrPath: script.src assignments and generic loadScript/load_with_timeout patterns in code-webflow references
searchActions.result: Found one unconstrained generic loader and one HTTPS/host-allowlisted counterexample.
The added usage wrapper broadly recommends the patterns without documenting the loader's trusted-input boundary.

searchLedger.rationale: The current in-scope wrapper makes a security-sensitive pre-existing pattern consumable without its trust precondition; no executable path supports severity above advisory.

searchLedger.invariant: Cookie, browser storage, and authorization guidance must distinguish client and server trust boundaries.

searchActions.result: Client-set cookies are limited to non-sensitive values; session cookies are server-set HttpOnly; sensitive browser storage is labeled unsafe.

searchLedger.rationale: The formerly disclosed cookie contradiction is not present in the current file.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Current text explicitly separates non-sensitive client cookies from sensitive server-set session cookies.

searchLedger.invariant: Dangerous eval, DOM-sink, shell-input, and hardcoded-secret examples must not be presented as safe recommendations.

searchActions.queryOrPath: eval, innerHTML, document.cookie, hardcoded secret, and shell input patterns in declared trees
searchActions.result: Focused occurrences were labeled bad/dangerous and paired with safe alternatives.

searchLedger.rationale: No inspected dangerous sink or secret literal was framed as recommended production behavior.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Bad examples carry explicit negative labels and safer alternatives.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The summary's disclosed arbitrary-input third-party loader concern remains active in the current target.

searchActions.queryOrPath: implementation-summary follow-up versus current best-practices loader
searchActions.result: The summary prose is stale; the current loader validates HTTPS, allowlists hosts, and notes SRI.

searchLedger.rationale: Implementation evidence outranks stale summary prose for current security behavior.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The current target contains the missing allowlist and integrity guidance.

searchLedger.invariant: The packet must not introduce executable tooling, runtime capability, or a falsely claimed executable security gate.

searchActions.queryOrPath: packet scope, technical context, and security checklist
searchActions.result: The declared and reviewed target consists of Markdown structural changes with no executable/tooling surface.

searchLedger.rationale: No executable capability was introduced; P2-001 concerns documentation recommendation framing only.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: All in-scope deliverables are Markdown references/assets and packet documentation.
